zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Howard於1376年出生在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 格曼斯時，她的父親Sir Robert Howard40歲，母親Margaret Scales41歲。她於1390年2月之後與Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron在英國諾福爾可巴肯哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1433年3月25日在英國諾福爾可諾維其享年57歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可諾維其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Howard于1376年出生在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 格曼斯时，她的父亲Sir Robert Howard40岁，母亲Margaret Scales41岁。她于1390年2月之后与Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron在英国諾福爾可巴肯哈姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1433年3月25日在英国諾福爾可諾維其享年57岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Howard was born in 1376, in Wiggenhall St Germans, Norfolk, England, her father, Sir Robert Howard, was 40 and her mother, Margaret Scales, was 41. She married Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron after February 1390, in Buckenham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 25 March 1433, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Howard est née en 1376, à Wiggenhall St Germans, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Howard, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Margaret Scales, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron après février 1390, à Buckenham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 25 mars 1433, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Howardが1376年にイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ジャーマンスで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Howardは40歳，母Margaret Scalesは41歳であった。1390年2月以後にイギリスノーフォルクブクエンハムでSir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1433年3月25日にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで57歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクノーウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Howard(이)가 1376년 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 그먼스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Howard(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Scales(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1390년 2월 이후 영국 노르폴크 벅함에서 Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1433년 3월 25일 영국 노르폴크 노위치에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Howard nasceu em 1376, em Wiggenhall St Germans, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Howard, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Scales, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron após fevereiro de 1390, em Buckenham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 25 de março de 1433, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Howard родилась в 1376 г. в местности вигенолл ст джирманс, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Howard было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Scales, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Constantine de Clifton 2nd Baron после февраль 1390 г. в местности буккенам, норфолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 25 марта 1433 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия.
